text.title: Kubernetes Deployment Strategies Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the kubernetes-deployment methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Recreate is only acceptable for stateful singletons; stateless services MUST use Rolling Update, Blue-Green or Canary.
rule.rationale: Recreate causes a downtime window every deploy — unacceptable for any user-facing service.

rule.statement: For Rolling Update, set maxSurge and maxUnavailable explicitly (do not rely on defaults) sized to the replica count and to PDB minAvailable.
rule.rationale: Default 25%/25% breaks PDB on small replica counts; explicit values keep rollout safe.

rule.statement: Blue-Green deployments MUST flip traffic via service selector or weighted ingress with a documented rollback step that flips back in < 60 seconds.
rule.rationale: Without a fast, single-action rollback, Blue-Green loses the only property that justifies its cost.

rule.statement: Canary deployments MUST attach metric-based analysis (Argo Rollouts AnalysisTemplate, Flagger metric refs) that auto-aborts on threshold breach.
rule.rationale: Time-based canaries miss regressions that show up under real traffic; metric analysis is what makes canary safe.

rule.statement: Rollback procedure is documented per service AND exercised at least quarterly in a non-trivial scenario.
rule.rationale: Rollback that has never been run is theatre — the quarterly drill is what proves it works.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
